question_text: Two light bulbs are connected to the same 230 V power supply. Bulb X has a power rating of 60 W, and Bulb Y has a power rating of 100 W. Which statement best explains why Bulb Y is brighter?

options.label: A
options.text: Bulb Y transfers more electrical energy to light energy per second than Bulb X.

options.label: B
options.text: Bulb Y uses a higher voltage than Bulb X.

options.label: C
options.text: Bulb Y has a lower resistance, so it is brighter.

options.label: D
options.text: Bulb Y transfers energy for a longer time than Bulb X.

explanation: Power is the rate of energy transfer per second. Since Bulb Y has a higher power rating (100 W vs 60 W), it transfers more energy each second, producing more light and appearing brighter. Both bulbs experience the same voltage, so option B is incorrect. Brightness depends on power (rate), not resistance or duration.
